footer.brand_desc: The governance gateway for AI tools, agents, and LLMs, preventing leakage, producing proof, and enabling responsible AI.

cookie_banner.text: We use cookies to improve your experience. By continuing to visit this site you agree to our use of cookies.

home.meta_desc: Prunex helps enterprises govern AI with confidence through policy control, compliance support, responsible AI oversight, and auditability.
home.hero_desc: Prunex helps enterprises enforce policy, support compliance, and govern responsible AI across models, agents, and workflows.
home.stat_1_label: of enterprises report AI data leakage incidents
home.stat_2_label: average cost of a data breach in 2025
home.stat_3_label: of companies reported AI-related security incidents
home.stat_sources: Sources: Cyberhaven 2025 Enterprise DLP Report · IBM/Ponemon Cost of a Data Breach 2025 · HiddenLayer AI Threat Landscape 2025
home.core_1_desc: Define, version, and enforce AI usage policies consistently across your organisation's systems and teams.
home.core_1_example: Example: Define who can access which AI models, with what data, under what conditions.
home.core_2_desc: Map governance controls to regulatory requirements and generate structured evidence for audits and reviews.
home.core_2_example: Example: Auto-generate audit trails aligned to EU AI Act, GDPR, HIPAA, NIST AI RMF, or ISO 42001.
home.core_3_desc: Embed fairness, transparency, and ethical oversight into AI operations as standard practice, not an afterthought.
home.core_3_example: Example: Detect and flag biased outputs, block sensitive data exposure, log every decision.
home.landscape_desc: Enterprises are deploying AI at pace. But governance has not kept up. Without structured controls, organisations face regulatory risk, reputational exposure, and operational gaps they cannot easily close later.
home.landscape_1_desc: AI is moving from experiments to production across every enterprise function. Governance must be operational, not theoretical.
home.landscape_2_desc: Most organisations rely on scattered policies, manual reviews, and team-level controls. This creates blind spots and inconsistency.
home.landscape_3_desc: Regulators, boards, and customers expect verifiable AI governance. Written policies alone are no longer sufficient.
home.landscape_4_desc: Bias, fairness, and transparency are board-level concerns. Organisations need structured ways to monitor and enforce ethical standards.
home.cost_1_desc: Non-compliance penalties grow as AI regulation matures globally.
home.cost_2_desc: Without governance, compliance and security teams block production rollouts.
home.cost_3_desc: Uncontrolled AI outputs can cause real harm and public trust erosion.
home.urgent_desc: These are real incidents that impacted real enterprises. Each could have been prevented with proper AI policy enforcement.
home.case_1_desc: Samsung employees pasted proprietary semiconductor source code and internal meeting recordings into ChatGPT. Confidential data entered a third-party AI system with no retrieval mechanism.
home.case_1_prevention: Policy-based controls that detect and block sensitive data before it reaches external AI systems. Every interaction inspected, evaluated, and logged.
home.case_2_desc: A bug in ChatGPT's infrastructure exposed chat histories and payment information of 1.2% of Plus subscribers, including names, email addresses, and partial credit card numbers.
home.case_2_prevention: Continuous monitoring of AI system outputs for PII exposure. Automated redaction and audit logging of every data flow.
home.case_3_desc: 68% of enterprises report AI data leakage from employees sharing sensitive information with AI tools. Only 23% have comprehensive security policies addressing these risks.
home.case_3_prevention: Enterprise-wide governance layer that enforces policy across every AI interaction, regardless of the tool or model employees use.
home.how_desc: Prunex applies a structured governance process to every AI interaction in your enterprise.
home.step_1_desc: Analyse prompts, responses, data flows, and tool calls across AI systems in real time.
home.step_2_desc: Assess each interaction against your defined policies, rules, and compliance requirements.
home.step_3_desc: Apply actions automatically: allow, block, redact, or flag based on policy outcomes.
home.step_4_desc: Record every decision with full context. Export structured evidence for reviews and regulators.
home.arch_desc: Prunex operates as a governance layer between your enterprise environment and AI systems.
home.arch_note: Works with Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, internal LLMs, custom agents, and any AI system in your environment.
home.who_desc: Prunex is designed for the teams and leaders responsible for safe, compliant AI adoption.
home.who_1_desc: Enforce data handling policies and maintain audit-ready evidence across all AI systems.
home.who_2_desc: Scale AI adoption with built-in governance. Clear boundaries that enable faster, safer deployment.
home.who_3_desc: Healthcare, financial services, legal, insurance, and critical infrastructure. Governance that meets sector requirements.
home.who_4_desc: Integrate AI governance into enterprise transformation programmes from the start, not after the fact.
home.fw_title: Built for the frameworks that matter
home.fw_desc: Prunex is designed to support governance requirements across major regulatory and compliance frameworks.
home.fw_euai_desc: Risk classification, transparency obligations, and documentation requirements for AI systems deployed in or serving the EU.
home.fw_gdpr_desc: Data protection and privacy requirements for AI systems processing personal data of EU residents, including automated decision-making safeguards.
home.fw_hipaa_desc: Safeguards for AI systems handling protected health information, supporting compliance with healthcare data privacy and security requirements.
home.fw_nist_desc: Structured approach to AI risk management aligned with the NIST Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework.
home.fw_iso_desc: Support for organisations pursuing AI management system certification under the ISO/IEC 42001 standard.
home.fw_soc_desc: Audit-ready evidence and controls documentation to support SOC 2 trust service criteria for AI operations.
home.diff_desc: What makes our approach to AI governance different.
home.diff_1: Policy-first architecture designed for enterprise complexity
home.diff_2: Audit-grade evidence generated automatically with every action
home.diff_3: Responsible AI embedded into operations, not bolted on
home.diff_4: Observe-first rollout model that reduces adoption risk
home.diff_5: Integrates with existing identity, logging, and SIEM infrastructure
home.diff_6: Governance as an enabler of AI adoption, not a blocker
home.cta_title: Ready to govern AI with confidence?
home.cta_desc: See how Prunex can support your organisation's AI governance requirements with a free compliance assessment or interactive demo.

about.intro: Prunex exists to help organisations adopt AI with stronger control, clearer accountability, and greater trust. We build governance infrastructure that makes AI safer to deploy, simpler to oversee, and easier to prove compliant.
about.mission_desc: We help enterprises govern AI with confidence by providing structured policy control, continuous compliance support, and responsible AI oversight. Our mission is to turn governance from a set of principles into an operational discipline that works at enterprise scale.
about.vision_desc: We envision a future where organisations use AI at scale without sacrificing policy, trust, compliance, or responsibility. Where governance is not a barrier to innovation but the foundation that makes it sustainable, accountable, and worthy of confidence.
about.why_desc: The AI adoption curve is outpacing the governance infrastructure needed to support it.
about.problem_1_desc: Enterprises are deploying AI models, agents, and workflows across every function. The pace is increasing, but governance structures remain static and manual.
about.problem_2_desc: Policies sit in documents. Oversight is team-by-team. There is no single layer ensuring consistent control across AI systems, data flows, and decision points.
about.problem_3_desc: Regulators expect proof. Boards expect accountability. Customers expect transparency. Organisations need infrastructure that delivers all three, continuously.
about.leadership_title: Built by people who understand the problem
about.leadership_desc_1: Prunex was founded by practitioners who have worked at the intersection of enterprise technology, AI systems, and regulatory compliance. We understand what it takes to build governance that operates in production, not just on paper.
about.leadership_desc_2: Our leadership team brings deep experience across enterprise software, data governance, policy frameworks, and AI engineering. We have built and deployed systems at scale, and we know where governance breaks down when it is not designed into the architecture.
about.principle_1: Governance must be operational, not theoretical
about.principle_3: Responsible AI starts at the infrastructure layer
about.values_title: Principles that guide everything we build
about.value_1_desc: Governance only works if stakeholders trust it. We build for transparency, traceability, and verifiability.
about.value_2_desc: Complex systems require clear communication. We make governance understandable for every stakeholder.
about.value_3_desc: Responsible AI is not optional. We embed ethical oversight and fairness into the operational layer.
about.value_4_desc: Enterprises need enforceable control, not suggestions. We build systems that apply policy, not just document it.
about.value_5_desc: We design for real enterprise environments. Our solutions work within existing architectures and timelines.
about.cta_title: Let's talk about trusted AI governance
about.cta_desc: We would welcome the opportunity to understand your governance challenges and share how Prunex can help.

offerings.hero_desc: Prunex helps enterprises strengthen AI governance through structured policy control, compliance support, responsible AI oversight, and continuous auditability. Each capability is designed to work independently or as part of a unified governance programme.
offerings.o1_desc: Prunex provides a centralised policy control layer that helps enterprises govern AI interactions consistently across teams, systems, and environments.
offerings.o1_f1: Centralised policy definitions with versioning and change tracking
offerings.o1_f2: Governance applied across prompts, responses, tool calls, and data flows
offerings.o1_f3: Strengthened oversight across enterprise AI usage, from copilots to agent-based systems
offerings.o1_f4: Scope-based controls by team, environment, use case, or risk level
offerings.o1_example_desc: No customer PII in AI prompts. No medical advice without human review. No model access without role-based authorisation. No sensitive data sent to third-party AI tools.
offerings.o2_desc: Prunex helps organisations improve governance visibility and maintain structured, audit-ready records of AI system operations and policy enforcement.
offerings.o2_f1: Improved governance visibility across AI interactions and decision points
offerings.o2_f2: Structured audit-ready records with full decision context and policy references
offerings.o2_f3: Support for accountability programs and internal compliance reviews
offerings.o2_f4: Exportable evidence packages for regulators and external auditors
offerings.o2_example_desc: EU AI Act, GDPR, HIPAA, NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001, SOC 2, and sector-specific regulatory requirements.
offerings.o3_desc: Prunex helps enable stronger ethical and governance safeguards across AI operations, supporting detection of problematic outputs and continuous oversight.
offerings.o3_f1: Support for detecting bias, discrimination, and problematic AI outputs
offerings.o3_f2: Strengthened ethical and governance safeguards embedded in standard operations
offerings.o3_f3: Continuous oversight of AI behaviour, decisions, and outputs
offerings.o3_f4: Configurable thresholds and escalation workflows for policy violations
offerings.o3_example_desc: Biased hiring recommendations. Sensitive medical data in outputs. Hallucinated legal citations. Discriminatory language patterns.
offerings.uc_desc: Prunex supports governance across a range of enterprise AI deployments.
offerings.uc_1_desc: Govern copilots and assistants that access internal data and systems
offerings.uc_2_desc: Policy controls for developer, legal, and HR AI tools
offerings.uc_3_desc: Ensure chatbots and AI interfaces meet compliance standards
offerings.uc_4_desc: Govern automated decision-making and AI-driven processes
offerings.uc_5_desc: Oversight for autonomous AI agents with tool access
offerings.fit_title: How Prunex fits into your environment
offerings.fit_desc: Prunex operates as a governance layer between enterprise users, applications, and AI capabilities. It integrates into existing architectures without requiring changes to underlying AI systems or workflows.
offerings.fit_1_desc: Prunex works alongside existing identity, logging, and infrastructure systems.
offerings.fit_2_desc: Governance applied consistently regardless of the AI model, provider, or framework in use.
offerings.fit_3_desc: Start with one use case. Expand governance across teams and environments as AI usage grows.
offerings.cta_title: Explore how Prunex can fit your AI environment
offerings.cta_desc: Contact our team to discuss your governance requirements and see how our offerings align with your needs.

contact.hero_desc: Let's talk about your AI governance needs.
contact.ph_message: Tell us about your governance needs or questions
contact.privacy_notice: By submitting this form, you agree that we may process your information to respond to your inquiry. See our
contact.success_desc: We've received your message and will get back to you soon.
contact.error_desc: We could not send your message. Please try again or email us directly at n.khamash@prunex.ai.
contact.sidebar_next_desc: Once you submit your inquiry, a member of our team will review it and respond within one to two business days. We may schedule a brief introductory call to better understand your governance requirements.

why_prunex.hero_desc: Governing AI at enterprise scale requires more than fragmented controls, vendor-specific tools, or manual processes. It requires purpose-built governance infrastructure that works across systems, teams, and regulatory environments.
why_prunex.diff_1_desc: Governance starts with structured, versioned policies. Prunex organises controls around your specific policies, standards, and risk appetite rather than relying on generic rule sets.
why_prunex.diff_2_desc: Ethical oversight is embedded into the governance framework. Bias detection, fairness monitoring, and transparency reporting operate as standard controls, not as separate initiatives.
why_prunex.diff_3_desc: Designed for organisations that need verifiable proof of governance. Every enforcement action, every decision, every exception is recorded with full context and traceability.
why_prunex.diff_4_desc: Prunex applies governance consistently across AI models, agents, workflows, and tools regardless of vendor or framework. One governance layer for your entire AI environment.
why_prunex.diff_5_desc: Structured, audit-ready evidence is generated automatically. Compliance teams gain visibility without manual evidence collection or retrospective documentation assembly.
why_prunex.diff_6_desc: Prunex integrates into existing enterprise architectures and supports an observe-first rollout model. Start with visibility before activating enforcement.
why_prunex.comp_title: How Prunex compares to common approaches
why_prunex.comp_desc: Most organisations rely on approaches that were not designed for cross-system AI governance.
why_prunex.now_title: The window for proactive governance is narrowing
why_prunex.now_desc: Organisations that build governance infrastructure now will be better positioned for what comes next.
why_prunex.now_1_desc: Enterprises are moving AI from experimentation to production across every function. Each new deployment increases the surface area that governance must cover. Retrofitting controls later is significantly harder and more expensive.
why_prunex.now_2_desc: The EU AI Act entered into force in August 2024, with compliance obligations phasing in through 2025 and 2026. GDPR requirements for automated decision-making, HIPAA safeguards for AI in healthcare, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, and sector-specific regulations are creating concrete obligations. Organisations need systems that produce evidence, not just commitments.
why_prunex.now_3_desc: The average cost of a data breach now exceeds $5.1 million. 77% of companies report at least one AI-related security incident. Boards, customers, regulators, and the public expect organisations to demonstrate responsible AI use through verifiable control and consistent accountability.
why_prunex.cta_title: Build AI governance on stronger foundations
why_prunex.cta_desc: Contact our team to discuss how Prunex can support your organisation's governance requirements.

blog.intro: Real-world lessons from AI data leakage incidents, governance failures, and the growing case for structured oversight across enterprise AI systems.
blog.b1_title: The ChatGPT Bug That Exposed User Payment Data
blog.b1_desc: A bug in an open-source library caused ChatGPT to leak chat histories and payment details of 1.2% of Plus subscribers. What happened, and what it means for AI platform security.
blog.b2_title: How Samsung Employees Leaked Source Code to ChatGPT
blog.b2_desc: Three separate incidents where Samsung engineers pasted proprietary code and internal meeting recordings into ChatGPT. A lesson in the human factor of AI data leakage.
blog.b3_title: The $200 Attack That Extracted Training Data from ChatGPT
blog.b3_desc: Researchers from Google DeepMind discovered that repeating single words could coax ChatGPT into leaking memorised training data, including personal information and code.
blog.cta_title: Don't let your organisation be the next case study
blog.cta_desc: See how Prunex helps enterprises prevent AI data leakage through policy control, compliance support, and real-time oversight.

demo.sidebar_desc: Select an LLM, then start a governed chat session.
demo.guard_note: Messages containing sensitive data are blocked before reaching the model.
demo.welcome_msg: Welcome. Choose ChatGPT, Gemini, or Claude from the left to start.
demo.select_first: Select a model first to start.
demo.blocked_msg: Blocked: this message contains sensitive data (email or phone number). Remove it and try again.
demo.reply_pricing: Pricing depends on scope and controls. Use Contact for a tailored plan.
demo.reply_compliance: Prunex maps controls to EU AI Act, GDPR, HIPAA, NIST AI RMF, and ISO 42001.
demo.reply_demo: You can request a personalized demo from the Contact page.
demo.reply_default: Message received. This is a demo response path for the full-page chat experience.
demo.attachment_reply: File received in demo mode. Connect backend storage to process attachments.
demo.error_sending: Unexpected error while sending chat message.

assessment.landing_title: How ready is your organisation for AI and data compliance?
assessment.landing_subtitle: Get a personalised readiness score across 13 regulatory frameworks in under 10 minutes.
assessment.disclaimer: This assessment is indicative only and does not constitute legal advice or compliance certification.
assessment.results_disclaimer: This assessment provides an indicative readiness overview only. It does not constitute legal advice, a compliance certification, or a formal audit. Consult qualified legal and compliance professionals for formal assessments.
assessment.question: Question
assessment.fw_title: Which frameworks are relevant to your organisation?
assessment.fw_desc: Based on your profile, we've highlighted recommended frameworks. Select up to 3.
assessment.resume_desc: You have an assessment in progress
assessment.intake_q2: How many employees does your organisation have?
assessment.intake_q3: How would you describe your current AI governance maturity?
assessment.intake_q3_o2: Early stage: some policies exist but informal
assessment.classify_q1: Does your organisation develop or deploy AI systems?
assessment.classify_q1_o1: We develop AI (build models or AI-powered products)
assessment.classify_q1_o2: We deploy AI (use AI systems built by third parties)
assessment.classify_q1_o4: Neither, we only use consumer AI tools
assessment.classify_q2: Do any of your AI systems operate in these areas?
assessment.classify_q3: Do any AI systems you use perform any of the following?
assessment.classify_q3_o1: Manipulate people's behaviours without their awareness
assessment.classify_q3_o3: Score citizens for social compliance purposes
assessment.classify_q4: Do you use General Purpose AI models such as ChatGPT API, Claude API, Gemini API, or open-source models like Llama?
assessment.classify_q4_o1: Yes, we build products on top of them
assessment.classify_q4_o2: Yes, employees use them directly for work tasks
assessment.classify_q5: How many employees regularly use AI tools in daily work?
assessment.skipped_msg: Skipped based on your previous answer
assessment.lead_desc_full: Enter your details to view your personalised compliance readiness report.
assessment.lead_consent: I agree to Prunex's Privacy Policy and consent to being contacted about my assessment results.
assessment.lead_marketing: I'd like to receive AI governance insights and updates from Prunex.
assessment.lead_error: Please complete all required fields and accept the privacy policy.
assessment.lead_email_error: Please enter a valid work email address.
assessment.results_next_desc: Our team can help you close these gaps with tailored policy frameworks and implementation support.

eu_ai_act.description: EU Artificial Intelligence Act - risk-based governance framework for AI systems
eu_ai_act.penalty: Up to €35M or 7% of global turnover

gdpr.description: General Data Protection Regulation - personal data protection for EU operations
gdpr.penalty: Up to €20M or 4% of global turnover

gdpr_bdsg.description: GDPR with German BDSG additions - works council, employee data, DPO thresholds
gdpr_bdsg.penalty: Same as GDPR plus BDSG-specific fines

iso_42001.description: ISO/IEC 42001 - AI Management System certification standard

soc_2.description: SOC 2 Type II - security trust standard for SaaS and service organisations
soc_2.urgency: Market requirement for US enterprise sales

dora.description: Digital Operational Resilience Act - ICT risk for financial entities
dora.penalty: Up to 1% of average daily global turnover

nis2.description: NIS2 Directive - cybersecurity for essential and important entities
nis2.penalty: Up to €10M or 2% of global turnover

eu_mdr.description: EU Medical Device Regulation - for AI-powered medical devices and SaMD

diga.description: German Digital Health Applications - BfArM listing for GKV reimbursement
diga.penalty: Denial of listing; loss of reimbursement

khzg.description: Hospital Future Act - IT security and digitalisation for German hospitals

hipaa.description: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act - US health data privacy
hipaa.penalty: Up to $1.5M per violation category per year

pci_dss.description: PCI DSS v4.0 - payment card data security standard
pci_dss.penalty: Fines up to $500K/month; loss of processing

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.settings_desc: Manage your cookie preferences below. Essential cookies cannot be disabled as they are required for the website to function.
cookies.essential_desc: Required for core website functionality. Always active.
cookies.analytics_desc: Help us understand how visitors use our website to improve the experience.
cookies.marketing_desc: Used to deliver relevant advertisements and track campaign performance.
